items.title: Average annual economic losses from wildfires in #OECD countries nearly quadrupled between 2000-2014 and 2015-2024. Natu...
items.content_text:
Average annual economic losses from wildfires in #OECD countries nearly quadrupled between 2000-2014 and 2015-2024.
 Natural hazards, such as wildfires, can lead to huge financial costs that many individuals, households or businesses may struggle to cover.
 Insurance can provide an important source of funding to absorb these costs and support recovery.
 But with increasing wildfire risks driven by changes in land-use and weather conditions, economic losses are dramatically increasing, affecting access to affordable property insurance coverage in several regions.
 How can governments, insurance regulators and the insurance sector respond to these risks to ensure people are financially protected?
 Find out in the OECD report on Financial Protection Against Catastrophic Risks ➡️ https://brnw.ch/21x4eZ1

items.title: We recently hosted a Lunch & Learn in partnership with Kubota Tractor Corporation to showcase the latest innovations in...
items.content_text:
We recently hosted a Lunch & Learn in partnership with Kubota Tractor Corporation to showcase the latest innovations in Kubota equipment to improve efficiency, safety, and productivity in the field for CHAPublicWorks employees as well as other attendees from surrounding local municipalities at the quarterly APWA meeting. The presentation highlighted features and engineering improvements that lend toward increased operator comfort, improved visibility, simplified maintenance access, advanced hydraulic systems, and integrated technology to maximize uptime and reduce operating costs. 
 Kubota equipment showcased included:
 🔸KX080-5 
 🔸SVL97-3 
 🔸RTV-X Long Bed
 🔸Tractor Mid-Mount Boom Mower
 Participants also had the opportunity to climb into the operator stations, discuss real-world municipal applications, and compare equipment capabilities based on operational needs with an on site demonstration in the equipment yard. 
 This event reinforces the importance of strong partnerships between equipment providers, manufacturers, and Public Works agencies to provide valuable insight into equipment selection and best practices that support efficient municipal operations. We appreciate the opportunity to highlight Kubota equipment features and capabilities to the City of Chattanooga Public Works team, and we look forward to growing our partnership with them for many years in the future! 
 #kubotausa #chattanoogapublicworks #kubotaofchattanooga

items.title: Do your elected officials represent your values? That's a question only you can answer, but it starts with knowing the d...
items.content_text:
Do your elected officials represent your values?
 That's a question only you can answer, but it starts with knowing the decisions they're making.
 From budgets and school policies to healthcare, housing, elections, and public safety, our elected officials make choices that shape our community every day. Those choices should reflect the values and priorities of those they seek to represent. 
 This post isn't about telling you what to think. It's about encouraging you to ask questions.
 ✔ What decisions have your elected officials made?
 ✔ Why did they make them?
 ✔ Do those decisions align with your values?
 An informed vote starts long before Election Day! 
 Early voting starts TODAY for our County General and State Primary Election! 
 Check out our School Board Voter Guide and Pledge to Vote on our website: www.calebcha.org/VOTE

items.title: Leaders from across Tennessee gathered to launch TN QuantumWorks, a first-of-its-kind K-12 quantum education initiative...
items.content_text:
Leaders from across Tennessee gathered to launch TN QuantumWorks, a first-of-its-kind K-12 quantum education initiative that will help prepare students for the jobs of tomorrow.
 Mayor Weston Wamp joined Mayor Tim Kelly, Senator Bo Watson, Hamilton County Schools, UTC, EPB, and community partners to celebrate Hamilton County’s role as the pilot district for this groundbreaking program.
 A special thank you to Dr. Dane and Sheila Boyington of Thinking Media for their vision and leadership in developing the innovative curriculum that will introduce students to quantum technology and future career opportunities.

items.title: The world is watching Spain — and now they're watching Chattanooga! Since January and the announcement of being Spain's...
items.content_text:
The world is watching Spain — and now they're watching Chattanooga! Since January and the announcement of being Spain's host city, we have talked to news outlets across the globe, been mentioned on social media, and had "Chattanooga" spoken corners of the world for the very first time. These news clips are just a small glimpse into the conversation.
 The $600,000 we spent from hotel-motel tax for Spain's security has generated over $100 million in ad value. While this remarkable return on our investment is worthy of celebration, the real excitement comes from knowing we have succeeded in getting the world talking about us. It might be about fútbol right now, but this momentum is how we make waves as a city — attracting visitors, advocating for funds that can make everyday life here better, and showing folks that we are not just some small town!

items.title: CPD Arrests Suspect in Manor Road Homicide The Chattanooga Police Department (CPD) arrested a man in connection to a hom...
items.content_text:
CPD Arrests Suspect in Manor Road Homicide
 The Chattanooga Police Department (CPD) arrested a man in connection to a homicide at a Manor Road residence.
 On July 16, 2026 at 3:32 p.m., CPD officers responded to a call regarding a deceased man in the 3900 block of Manor Road. 
 Upon arrival, police found the 53-year-old victim inside a residence with an injury to the back of his head.
 CPD's Homicide Unit responded to the scene to conduct the investigation and subsequently identified and arrested the suspect, 37-year-old Nathaniel Colvin. Colvin is charged with first-degree murder, especially aggravated robbery, and tampering with evidence. He is in custody at the Hamilton County Jail and Detention Center. 
 The information is preliminary and is subject to change as the investigation continues. Chattanooga Police ask anyone with information regarding this incident to call 423-698-2525.
 The charges and allegations referenced in this release are merely accusations of criminal conduct and not evidence. A defendant is presumed innocent unless and until proven guilty beyond a reasonable doubt and convicted through due process of law.
 #CPDnews

items.title: 🌿 Preserve the Land. Protect the Play. 🌿 Wildwood Playscape was created to inspire imagination, adventure, and a love...
items.content_text:
🌿 Preserve the Land. Protect the Play. 🌿
 Wildwood Playscape was created to inspire imagination, adventure, and a love for nature. Every log to climb, every trail to explore, and every space to discover exists because our community values places where families can connect and children can simply be kids.
 Recently, Wildwood experienced vandalism. It's incredibly disappointing to see a place built for everyone intentionally damaged, especially one that has quickly become a favorite destination for so many families.
 But we also know this community.
 Time and again, we've seen Hamilton County rally around the places, people, and causes it believes in. We know Wildwood is no different.
 We're asking everyone who visits to help us protect this special place. Respect the space, encourage the next generation to do the same, and if you see vandalism or destructive behavior, please report it. Together, we can ensure Wildwood remains a place of adventure, wonder, and lasting memories for generations to come.
 💚 Preserve the Land. Protect the Play.
 #ProtectThePlay #WildwoodPlayscape #EnterpriseSouth #HamiltonCountyParks

Respect the space, encourage the next generation to do the same, and if you see vandalism or destructive behavior, please report it. Together, we can ensure Wildwood remains a place of adventure, wonder, and lasting memories for generations to come.
💚 Preserve the Land. Protect the Play.
#ProtectThePlay #WildwoodPlayscape #EnterpriseSouth #HamiltonCountyParks

items.title: Meet the inaugural Hamilton County–East Ridge Sports Authority. Hamilton County appointees Dane Bradshaw, Katie Burrows,...
items.content_text:
Meet the inaugural Hamilton County–East Ridge Sports Authority.
 Hamilton County appointees Dane Bradshaw, Katie Burrows, Hiren Desai, and Orlando Lightfoot will serve alongside East Ridge appointees Zach Hutcherson, Brad Jackson, Ken Meyer, and PJ Patel, with Emerson Russell serving as the joint appointee.
 The Hamilton County Commission this week considered the resolution to establish the nine-member authority, which will guide the planning process by evaluating successful facility models, developing governance recommendations, and preparing a financing and operational plan before any construction moves forward.
 “The caliber of people serving on this authority—including former collegiate and professional athletes, business leaders, and hospitality experts—reflects our commitment to making Hamilton County a premier youth sports destination,” said Mayor Weston Wamp.

items.title: A new inclusive playground could soon become a reality on Signal Mountain. This week, Mayor Weston Wamp, District 2 Comm...
items.content_text:
A new inclusive playground could soon become a reality on Signal Mountain.
 This week, Mayor Weston Wamp, District 2 Commissioner Chip Baker, and county leaders proposed a $210,516 investment to help complete the Dahlia Playground at Althaus Park. Pending Hamilton County Commission approval, the funding will support the purchase of inclusive play equipment, creating a space where children of all abilities can play side by side.
 Inspired by Dahlia “Dollie” Harmon and her family’s vision for a more inclusive community, the project is another example of what strong public-private partnerships can accomplish for Hamilton County families.
 If approved on August 5, construction is expected to begin later this year. 🛝

items.title: RECALL ALERT: General Mills Issues Recall for Nearly 736,000 Pillsbury Rolls due to potential glass contamination. The i...
items.content_text:
RECALL ALERT: General Mills Issues Recall for Nearly 736,000 Pillsbury Rolls due to potential glass contamination.
 The issue was discovered internally. The cases are marketed to businesses rather than individual consumers.
 The recall includes 3,080 cases of Pillsbury Bread Rolls "Hard Roll Dough," each containing 180 rolls, for a total of 554,400 rolls, and 1,260 cases of Pillsbury Bread Rolls "Kaiser Roll Dough," each containing 144 rolls, for a total of 181,440 rolls.
 If you think you may have purchased either case of rolls, confirm by checking the UPC number on the package and the use-by date by visiting https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/ires/?Event=99293

items.title: Happy Friday, Chattanooga! ICYMI, It also happens to be Parks and Outdoors Day! We're incredibly lucky to live in a plac...
items.content_text:
Happy Friday, Chattanooga! ICYMI, It also happens to be Parks and Outdoors Day!
 We're incredibly lucky to live in a place where you can wrap up the workweek and immediately hit a hiking trail, launch a kayak, or just hang out under the trees at one of our many parks without leaving town. 
 Behind every beautiful green space, youth sports league, and outdoor adventure (just to name a few!)  is a massive team of people working their tails off. Our entire department is out there every single day doing the heavy lifting behind the scenes to keep this city green, safe, and active. We really can't thank our staff enough for the long hours and hard work they put in to make Chattanooga such an incredible place to live.
 But honestly, all that hard work doesn't mean much without you. A park is just empty grass until the community shows up. Thank you to everyone who fills up our neighborhood spaces, walks the greenways, plays on the courts, or just takes a quiet minute to sit by the river after a long week. You’re the reason we do this, and seeing Chattanooga outside enjoying what we steward is the best reward our team could ask for.
 Now we want to challenge you to fill the comments with local inspiration for the weekend. Drop a photo below of your favorite outdoor space in Chattanooga, and tell us where it is so your neighbors can check it out. Bonus points to those who get outside and visit a new spot this weekend! 
 Have a great weekend out there! ☀️🥾

What a week! The 10th cohort of the  Urban95 Academy has just concluded their residence week. 

In the past 5-years the programme has brought over 300 urban leaders representing 100 city teams from all over the world to London to study at the London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE).

29 urban officials from 10 cities arrived in London to take part in five days of intensive in-person teaching, workshops and site visits during the week-long residence in central London.

Nooga Has Heart: Youth Performance and Pathways is a talent competition for Chattanooga's next generation of performing artists!
 Produced by Fallon Clark, Executive Director of Noogavision Theatre and Performing Arts Co. and member of the 2026 cohort of the Emerging Producers Program, Nooga Has Heart aims to create access, visibility, and opportunity for emerging artists across many performing arts disciplines. 
 Young artists ages 13-21 are invited to audition! Selected participants will receive a spot to perform at Youth Performance & Pathways, participate in artist development workshops, have chances to connect with local arts professionals, and compete for the inaugural Performing Artist Pulse Awards. 
 Auditions close on June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM. Learn more at www.noogavisiontheatre.org 
 Photo Credit: Fallon Clark, Noogavision Theatre and Performing Arts Co.

items.title: To: Mayor Lloyd and Collegedale City Commissioners From: Jon Holmes, City Manager Date: 07/17/2026 RE: Weekly Update 06/...
items.content_text:
To: Mayor Lloyd and Collegedale City Commissioners
 From: Jon Holmes, City Manager
 Date: 07/17/2026
 RE: Weekly Update 06/22 – 06/26
 Administration:
 This Weekly Update contains information from last week in addition to the current week since a weekly update was not distributed last Friday due to the City Manager being out of the office to attend a Conference
 • City Manager attended the TN Municipal League Conference along with two Commissioners, Commissioner Howse and Commissioner Sadler.
 • Public Works Staff, Police Staff, Parks and Rec Staff and Admin Staff After Event Meeting to discuss the Freedom Fest and talk through issues and concerns and how to best address those items next year.
 • Requested feedback from Commissioners on issues or concerns they saw during Freedom Fest, and any feedback they received from constituents.
 • CM also met with East Tennessee Symphony Orchestra to discuss their event and get feedback from them on concerns or issues and how to address them in preparation for discussions with the Commission to set the dates for next year’s event
 • Worked with Parks and Recreation Manager to assemble a survey for the community to gather input and opinions about the Freedom Fest event.
 • Met with two developers regarding possible proposed retail developments within Collegedale
 • Met with City Attorney regarding various items
 • Organized Joint Training for the City Commission, Planning Commission, and Economic(Industrial) Development Board for information about the Infrastructure Development Districts economic development program that is authorized by state statute
 • The new Communications and Engagement Manager for the City, McKenna Wallace, will begin her duties on August 3rd; we will be posting information about McKenna next week and we look forward to working with her.
 • Contacted the State Tourism Office to inquire about the Tourism Enhancement Grant – this grant has been used by municipalities around the state to assist with the installation of wayfinding and community signage in their respective communities. The next round of funding will be announced soon and we are planning on submitting an application to assist with our budgeted wayfinding signage project.
 Collegedale Airport:
 • We responded to audit requests from TDOT for our yearly grant compliance.
 • We had only a procedural finding for a Master Services Agreement with our previous on-call engineer consultant, that expired last year. The contract did not contain required language for subcontracting services. We are pleased to report that operationally, everything else was found to be in compliance. 
 • We have been following up on requests from TDOT regarding a previously issued NPDES permit that was given for the last obstruction removal project a few years back, to verify if it is still active. If it isn’t, we will need to apply for a re-issuance. There are some loose ends to get sorted out before the obstruction removal can begin that we are working through. We have been working with Michael Baker International and TDOT Aeronautics on a State-wide Obstruction Clearing initiative that was applied for a few weeks ago. An environmental assessment was submitted to TDEC and we’re currently waiting for a response to proceed.
 • The Airport responded to some reports of issues with the McDonald Terrace gate malfunctioning. The issue appears to be addressed.
 • We submitted the approved work authorization for the PAPI design project. We currently are waiting on the grant paperwork to come through so we can present it to the Commission for
 approval.

To: Mayor Lloyd and Collegedale City Commissioners
From: Jon Holmes, City Manager
Date: 07/17/2026
RE: Weekly Update 06/22 – 06/26
Administration:
This Weekly Update contains information from last week in addition to the current week since a weekly update was not distributed last Friday due to the City Manager being out of the office to attend a Conference
• City Manager attended the TN Municipal League Conference along with two Commissioners, Commissioner Howse and Commissioner Sadler.
• Public Works Staff, Police Staff, Parks and Rec Staff and Admin Staff After Event Meeting to discuss the Freedom Fest and talk through issues and concerns and how to best address those items next year.
• Requested feedback from Commissioners on issues or concerns they saw during Freedom Fest, and any feedback they received from constituents.
• CM also met with East Tennessee Symphony Orchestra to discuss their event and get feedback from them on concerns or issues and how to address them in preparation for discussions with the Commission to set the dates for next year’s event
• Worked with Parks and Recreation Manager to assemble a survey for the community to gather input and opinions about the Freedom Fest event.
• Met with two developers regarding possible proposed retail developments within Collegedale
• Met with City Attorney regarding various items
• Organized Joint Training for the City Commission, Planning Commission, and Economic(Industrial) Development Board for information about the Infrastructure Development Districts economic development program that is authorized by state statute
• The new Communications and Engagement Manager for the City, McKenna Wallace, will begin her duties on August 3rd; we will be posting information about McKenna next week and we look forward to working with her.
• Contacted the State Tourism Office to inquire about the Tourism Enhancement Grant – this grant has been used by municipalities around the state to assist with the installation of wayfinding and community signage in their respective communities. The next round of funding will be announced soon and we are planning on submitting an application to assist with our budgeted wayfinding signage project.
Collegedale Airport:
• We responded to audit requests from TDOT for our yearly grant compliance.
• We had only a procedural finding for a Master Services Agreement with our previous on-call engineer consultant, that expired last year. The contract did not contain required language for subcontracting services. We are pleased to report that operationally, everything else was found to be in compliance.
• We have been following up on requests from TDOT regarding a previously issued NPDES permit that was given for the last obstruction removal project a few years back, to verify if it is still active. If it isn’t, we will need to apply for a re-issuance. There are some loose ends to get sorted out before the obstruction removal can begin that we are working through. We have been working with Michael Baker International and TDOT Aeronautics on a State-wide Obstruction Clearing initiative that was applied for a few weeks ago. An environmental assessment was submitted to TDEC and we’re currently waiting for a response to proceed.
• The Airport responded to some reports of issues with the McDonald Terrace gate malfunctioning. The issue appears to be addressed.
• We submitted the approved work authorization for the PAPI design project. We currently are waiting on the grant paperwork to come through so we can present it to the Commission for
approval.

items.title: 🌿 Preserve the Land. Protect the Play. 🌿 Wildwood Playscape was created to inspire imagination, adventure, and a love...
items.content_text:
🌿 Preserve the Land. Protect the Play. 🌿
 Wildwood Playscape was created to inspire imagination, adventure, and a love for nature. Every log to climb, every trail to explore, and every space to discover exists because our community values places where families can connect and children can simply be kids.
 Recently, Wildwood experienced vandalism. It's incredibly disappointing to see a place built for everyone intentionally damaged, especially one that has quickly become a favorite destination for so many families.
 But we also know this community.
 Time and again, we've seen Hamilton County rally around the places, people, and causes it believes in. We know Wildwood is no different.
 We're asking everyone who visits to help us protect this special place. Respect the space, encourage the next generation to do the same, and if you see vandalism or destructive behavior, please report it. Together, we can ensure Wildwood remains a place of adventure, wonder, and lasting memories for generations to come.
 💚 Preserve the Land. Protect the Play.
 #ProtectThePlay #WildwoodPlayscape #EnterpriseSouth #HamiltonCountyParks

items.title: July is UV Safety Awareness Month, reminding us of the importance of protecting our skin when spending time outdoors.  W...
items.content_text:
July is UV Safety Awareness Month, reminding us of the importance of protecting our skin when spending time outdoors. 
 Whether you're at the pool, beach, hiking, watching a game at the ballpark, or enjoying a family outing, it's essential to use broad-spectrum sunscreen with SPF 30 or higher. 
 Additionally, consider wearing protective clothing and sunglasses, and seek shade during peak sun hours, which are typically between 10 A.M. and 4 P.M. By taking these simple precautions, you can reduce your risk of sunburn and long-term skin damage while still enjoying a fun and safe summer.
 The CDC recommends talking to your healthcare provider if you notice any changes in your skin, such as new or unusual moles, or changes in the size, shape, or color of existing ones. 
 Early detection can make a significant difference.

items.title: On a summer morning in the mountains of East Tennessee, a group of UTC students and researchers waded through the waters...
items.content_text:
On a summer morning in the mountains of East Tennessee, a group of UTC students and researchers waded through the waters of Bumbee Creek. 
 The group filled bottles with stream water and documented conditions at each site. Back on campus, those samples would help researchers identify pollutants that could be affecting the laurel dace, one of North America’s most endangered fish. 
 The project includes faculty and students from UTC’s Department of Chemistry and the Department of Biology, Geology, and Environmental Science, along with researchers from the Tennessee Aquarium Conservation Institute (TNACI).  
 🔗  Read more here: https://blog.utc.edu/…/saving-the-laurel-dace-students-and…/

items.content_text:
Did you know the Southeast Tennessee Tourism Association (SETTA) is one of the many programs and initiatives led by the Southeast Tennessee Development District (SETD)?

At SETTA, our mission is simple: We work with county leaders and tourism partners within the 10-county region of Southeast Tennessee, as well as with the Tennessee Department of Tourist Development, to promote economic development and regional prosperity through tourism, which is the second-largest industry in Tennessee.

Each month, our tourism newsletter highlights scenic outdoor adventures, local restaurants, hidden gems, festivals, family-friendly attractions, and unique experiences from across our 10-county region — all designed to inspire your next day trip or weekend getaway.

🏞️ Read this month's tourism newsletter: https://lnkd.in/eKnGWByR

📬 Want more Southeast Tennessee inspiration delivered to your inbox? Subscribe here: https://lnkd.in/e3U92Y7u 

Whether you're a lifelong resident or planning your next adventure, there's always something new to discover in Southeast Tennessee.

Each month, our tourism newsletter highlights scenic outdoor adventures, local restaurants, hidden gems, festivals, family-friendly attractions, and unique experiences from across our 10-county region — all designed to inspire your next day trip or weekend getaway. 🏞️ Read this month's tourism newsletter: https://lnkd.in/eKnGWByR 📬 Want more Southeast Tennessee inspiration delivered to your inbox? Subscribe here: https://lnkd.in/e3U92Y7u Whether you're a lifelong resident or planning your next adventure, there's always something new to discover in Southeast Tennessee.

items.title: How has productivity evolved since 1995? The #OECD Compendium of Productivity Indicators 2026 points to a mixed picture:...
items.content_text:
How has productivity evolved since 1995?
 The #OECD Compendium of Productivity Indicators 2026 points to a mixed picture: renewed labour productivity growth in 2024, but persistent long-term challenges in efficiency, technology diffusion and productivity gaps across firms and industries.
 Three takeaways stand out:
 ➡️ Labour productivity grew despite uncertainty: across the OECD, labour productivity increased by 1.2% in 2024, double the growth recorded in 2023. Gains were recorded in 29 countries, despite geopolitical tensions, skill shortages and elevated uncertainty.
 ➡️ National performances diverge further amid a long-term slowdown: virtually all OECD countries were more productive in 2024 than two decades earlier, but the pace of improvement has roughly halved. In 2024, labour productivity rose by 2.2% in the US, compared with 0.2% in the EU.
 ➡️ Aggregate trends mask large firm-level gaps: small and medium-sized enterprises' average labour productivity across OECD and accession candidate countries was only 65% of that of large businesses.
 Explore the latest OECD productivity insights: https://brnw.ch/21x4cFM
 #OECDstats

items.content_text:
❤️
Locations + more info in the caption ⬇️💦
 With the humid temps this summer (🥵), I thought it’s a good time to re-post this reel with free places to cool off when you’re visiting downtown Chattanooga, TN! 
 These are all great for local families with kids or those sightseeing/visiting while walking around Chattanooga! *Note: pets aren’t allowed in these public water areas
 📍 The Passage (aka the water steps) is an interactive art installation that’s a stairway waterfall cascading into a very shallow wading area next to the Tennessee Aquarium at Ross’s Landing.
 - FREE // pay to park
 - Beautiful riverfront views
 - Unique art honoring Native Americans of the Trail of Tears
 📍Coolidge Park (across the river) has a large fountain-style splash pad with animals that kids love.
 - FREE // pay to park
 - It’s located right next to the vintage carousel (free to ride!) and is a great walking/picnic area.
 - Lots of green space & great views of the river
 📍The Tennessee Aquarium Plaza has a wading stream and splash area with small rocks to climb around on.
 - FREE // pay to park
 - Located right outside of the aquarium (short walk from The Passage)
 - There are also walkways & bridges nearby
 #chattanooga #chattanoogatn #downtownchattanooga #chattanoogatennessee #tennessee

items.content_text:
⛑️ I may have missed media day… but I wasn’t going to miss the opportunity to see this incredible project up close! 😄
 ✅ Finally got my hard hat!
 ✅ Took an up-close tour of Chattanooga’s e2i2 Major Wastewater Infrastructure Investment 💧🏗️
 ✅ Got a behind-the-scenes look at one of the largest infrastructure investments in our city’s history.
 ✅ Learned how these improvements will modernize our wastewater system, protect our waterways, support future growth, and improve resiliency for generations to come.
 ✅ Walked away with an even greater appreciation for the talented professionals who keep our city running every single day.
 📸 Credit: Ayanna Bronner
 A huge THANK YOU to Chattanooga’s incredible Wastewater Team for taking the time to show me around and explain the work happening beneath our feet. Your expertise, passion, and commitment to serving Chattanooga do not go unnoticed!
 The best projects aren’t always the most visible—but they’re often the most important. Investing in infrastructure is investing in Chattanooga’s future, and I’m excited to see this transformational work continue.
 #HardHatTour ⛑️ #e2i2 #InfrastructureMatters #Wastewater #Chattanooga BuildingStrongerCommunities

items.title: Every semester, students at UTC purchase textbooks and course materials for their classes. Beginning this fall, they wil...
items.content_text:
Every semester, students at UTC purchase textbooks and course materials for their classes. Beginning this fall, they will have a new option designed to simplify the process and help reduce costs.
 Starting Monday, July 27, the MocsBooks Total Access Program will offer UTC undergraduate students a simpler, potentially more budget-friendly option for buying their textbooks and materials.
 Students will be automatically enrolled in the program and will receive an email 30 days before the start of the semester. The email will include the courses they are enrolled in, what the program covers and an estimate of how much they could save each semester.
 🔗  Learn more here: https://blog.utc.edu/…/introducing-mocsbooks-the-new-way-t…/

Chattanooga’s First Community Resource Hub Is Opening!
 The City of Chattanooga Department of Community Development invites you to celebrate the grand opening of the East Lake Community Resource Hub, the first of six neighborhood-based hubs planned across Chattanooga!
 📅 Monday, July 27, 2026
 🎀 Ribbon Cutting: 2:30 p.m.
 🎉 Community Celebration: 3:00–6:00 p.m.
 📍 East Lake Community Center (3601 Dodds Avenue, Chattanooga, TN 37407)
 🎟️Free and open to the public
 The East Lake Community Resource Hub will serve as a welcoming, one-stop destination where residents and families can connect with:
 ✅ Health and wellness services
 ✅ Educational resources
 ✅ Workforce development opportunities

items.title: Congratulations to St. Marks United Methodist Church on its listing in the National Register of Historic Places! 🎉 For...
items.content_text:
Congratulations to St. Marks United Methodist Church on its listing in the National Register of Historic Places! 🎉
 For more than 130 years, St. Marks has been a cornerstone of the North Chattanooga community—a place of worship, fellowship, and service. Founded in 1887, the congregation moved into its current sanctuary in 1913, famously marching through a snowstorm while singing "Onward Christian Soldiers" as they entered their new church home.
 Designed by architect W. H. Sears in the distinctive Spanish Mission style, the church remains one of Chattanooga's most architecturally significant religious landmarks. Its beautiful stucco exterior, prominent tower, and warm Mission-style interior reflect both exceptional craftsmanship and the vision of a community that came together to build a true "Neighborhood Church."
 Listing on the National Register of Historic Places recognizes not only the building's architectural significance but also the generations of people whose faith, generosity, and perseverance have shaped this remarkable place.
 Congratulations to the congregation of St. Marks United Methodist Church and to everyone who helped achieve this well-deserved recognition. Here's to preserving this important piece of Chattanooga's history for generations to come!
 #PreserveChattanooga #NationalRegister #HistoricPreservation #NorthChattanooga #ThisPlaceMatters #ChattanoogaHistory

items.title: Jon Holmes Joins Elite Group of ICMA-Credentialed City Managers in Tennessee WASHINGTON D.C. (July 16, 2026)
items.content_text:
Jon Holmes Joins Elite Group of ICMA-Credentialed City Managers in Tennessee
 WASHINGTON D.C. (July 16, 2026) – Jon Holmes, City Manager of the City of Collegedale, Tennessee, recently received the prestigious Credentialed Manager designation from ICMA, the International City/County Management Association. Mr. Holmes is one of over 1,700 local government management professionals currently credentialed through the ICMA Voluntary Credentialing Program.
 ICMA’s mission is to advance professional local government through leadership, management, innovation, and ethics and by increasing the proficiency of appointed chief administrative officers, assistant administrators, and other employees who serve local governments and regional entities around the world. The organization’s 13,000 members in 27 countries also include educators, students, and other local government employees.
 To receive the prestigious ICMA credential, a member must have significant experience as a senior management executive in local government; have earned a degree, preferably in public administration or a related field; and demonstrated a commitment to high standards of integrity and to lifelong learning and professional development.
 “On behalf of the City Board of Commissioners, we are proud to recognize Jon for earning the ICMA Credentialed Manager designation,” said Commissioner Debbie Baker. “This achievement reflects not only his efforts to work toward professional excellence, but also his deep commitment to ethical leadership, innovation, and continuous improvement in public service. Under Jon’s leadership, we are working to develop and address community priorities, strengthen our entire organization, and better serve all of our residents. This recognition demonstrates his ability to meet the high standard of governance and administration that our community expects and deserves”. Commissioner Laura Howse added “Understanding that only 24 city managers in Tennessee have earned this certification, makes me especially proud, good job Jon.”
 Mr. Holmes is qualified by 18+ years of professional local government executive experience, educational attainment of a Bachelor of Arts Degree from Harding University in History and Political Science and a Master’s in Public Administration from the University of Nebraska. Prior to his appointment in January 2026 as City Manager of Collegedale, he served as City Manager for the City of Aurora, Missouri for over 7 years, as well as serving as a County Administrator in Ottawa, Kansas, a City Administrator in Odessa, Missouri, and a Budget and Management Analyst for the City of Laramie Wyoming. Prior to that he worked as an Assistant Emergency Response and Management Coordinator for the Lincoln – Lancaster County Health Department in Lincoln, Nebraska. He has earned the Certified Municipal Official Certification from the Missouri Municipal League, has served as the Vice-Chair for the Transportation Advisory Committee with the Southwest Missouri Regional Planning Commission, served on the Judicial Nominating Commission for the Kansas 4th Judicial District, and has served on a number of regional planning commissions and other boards and commissions that work for the betterment of municipal governments and the citizens they serve.
 Mr. Holmes has also remained actively engaged in the communities he has served, contributing to organizations such as Rotary International, local chambers of commerce, and numerous other civic groups.
 The Credentialled City Manager designation from ICMA speaks to the high level of professionalism that Mr. Holmes brings to the City Manager position and also underscores the City of Collegedale’s commitment to recruiting and retaining highly qualified and experienced individuals to serve the Citizens of Collegedale.

items.title: This morning, Chief Deputy Spencer Daniels and HCSO personnel joined local first responders, military personnel, elected...
items.content_text:
This morning, Chief Deputy Spencer Daniels and HCSO personnel joined local first responders, military personnel, elected officials, and Hamilton County residents at the Fallen Five Memorial to remember the 11th anniversary of the July 16th, 2015, terrorist attack in Chattanooga. 
 The solemn ceremony served as a time of remembrance and reflection, honoring the lives of Marine Sergeant Carson Holmquist, Navy Logistics Specialist 2nd Class Randall Smith, Marine Gunnery Sergeant Thomas Sullivan, Marine Lance Corporal Squire “Skip” Wells, and Marine Staff Sergeant David Wyatt, who were killed during the terrorist attack at the Naval Reserve Center.
 Their legacy of service and sacrifice will forever be remembered.

items.title: Co-ordinated governance across water, energy, industry and urban development is essential to accelerate sustainable deve...
items.content_text:
Co-ordinated governance across water, energy, industry and urban development is essential to accelerate sustainable development.
 The new OECD report, Bridging the Gaps for Sustainable Development: Coherent Policies for Water, Energy, Industry and Cities, shows that these sectors are closely linked in practice, but are often planned and managed separately.
 Drawing on country experiences, the report highlights practical ways to strengthen policy coherence and co-ordination across levels of government, including:
 ➡️ better alignment between planning, budgeting and delivery
 ➡️ clearer institutional roles and responsibilities
 ➡️ more systematic use of data
 ➡️ greater capacity to manage trade-offs and spillovers
 The report also shows that integrated approaches can help governments reduce costs, strengthen resilience, and improve the effectiveness of policies across these areas.
 Download the report 🔗 https://brnw.ch/21x4bRv

items.content_text:
Traffic Advisory for Ringgold Road in #EastRidgeTN! 🚧
 Motorists traveling along Ringgold Road should expect traffic delays and changing traffic patterns as Tennessee American Water works on a new water main installation at the intersection of Adair Avenue and Ringgold Road.
 🚦 What to Expect:
 • Eastbound traffic begins merging into a single lane near Keeble Street.  An additional traffic shift occurs at Prater Road.  Traffic returns to normal at S. Smith Street.
 • Westbound traffic will not have access to the center turn lane beginning at Adair Avenue.  Traffic returns to normal at Prater Road.
 🚧 Additional Impacts:
 Temporary road closures and detours may occur on nearby residential streets, including Adair Avenue, Marietta Street, and S. Smith Street. 
 Please use caution when driving in the area.  The project is scheduled to be completed by the end of the business day on Friday, July 17, 2026.
 Thanks for your patience as these infrastructure improvements are completed and for your help in keeping everyone safe on the Ridge.

**Ready or Not! Back-to-School Season is Here!**
 As you prepare for the new school year by checking off school supplies, schedules, and paperwork, don't forget one important task: ensuring your child's required immunizations are up to date!
 Children entering preschool, pre-K, kindergarten, 7th grade, or those enrolling in a Tennessee school for the first time may need to obtain an updated Tennessee Certificate of Immunization.
 The Hamilton County Health Department offers childhood and school immunizations without an appointment. 
 School Shot Records can be requested via https://health.hamiltontn.org/data,…/requestshotrecords.aspx.
 For more information, please visit http://health.hamiltontn.org/…/childschoolimmunizations.aspx) or call (423) 209-8350 and press #2.

Over the next few weeks, we'd like to introduce you to the people behind the Southeast Tennessee Development District.

The work happening across our region is made possible by a dedicated team committed to serving our communities, building partnerships, and helping move Southeast Tennessee forward. We thought we'd start with the person you'll see (and hear from) the most on our communication channels. 

👋 Hi! I'm Chaz Johnston , Director of Communications & Community Relations at the Southeast Tennessee Development District (SETD).

Since joining the organization, I've had the opportunity to meet community leaders, partners, and organizations from across our 10-county region. And one thing has become abundantly clear to me: Southeast Tennessee is filled with passionate people who are investing their time, talent, and energy into making our communities stronger.

My role is to help tell those stories by showcasing the partnerships, projects, and people whose work often happens behind the scenes but creates a lasting impact throughout our region.

For me, communication is about more than sharing updates. It's about building trust, strengthening and cultivating relationships, and helping people see the value of working together toward a common goal.

Thank you for following along. Over the coming weeks, we'll be introducing you to the incredible people behind SETD and the work they're doing to help shape the future of Southeast Tennessee. Stay tuned. I can't wait for you to meet them!

 Sergeant Carson Holmquist, Gunnery Sergeant Thomas Sullivan, Staff Sergeant David Wyatt, Lance Corporal Skip Wells, and Petty Officer Second Class Randall Smith are honored here in Chattanooga every day at the Wreath of Honor Fallen Five Memorial. Our hearts are with their loved ones on this anniversary and we are thankful to continue to be a part of the annual wreath laying ceremony to commemorate their sacrifice.

items.title: The City of Collegedale is pleased to announce that Garrett Haynes has been selected as the new Planning & Community Dev...
items.content_text:
The City of Collegedale is pleased to announce that Garrett Haynes has been selected as the new Planning & Community Development Director. He will begin his role at the end of this month.
 Garrett is a 2015 graduate of the University of Tennessee at Chattanooga (UTC) and brings a diverse background in community planning, regional development, research, and geographic information systems. He joins us from the Southeast Tennessee Development District, where he has served for almost eight years, most recently as a Senior Planner. Prior to that, Garrett gained experience working with local and regional planning organizations in roles focused on GIS, research, and planning support, giving him valuable insight into the planning process and the opportunities and challenges communities face as they grow and develop.
 “We are excited to add Garrett to our Management Team," said City Manager Jon Holmes. "His experience will be a great asset, and the fact that he has worked in the Hamilton County area will help him get up to speed quickly with the great things that are happening in Collegedale."
 Outside of work, Garrett enjoys spending time on the golf course and fishing.
 We are excited to welcome Garrett and look forward to the experience and perspective he will bring to our leadership team!

items.title: From Metro Stations to Urban Places in Riyadh Can a metro station, on its own, create a vibrant, walkable neighbourhood? Twice a w...
items.content_text:
From Metro Stations to Urban Places in Riyadh

Can a metro station, on its own, create a vibrant, walkable neighbourhood?

Twice a week in the lead up to Graduation we’re showcasing the consultancy projects undertaken by participants on LSE Cities' Executive MSc in Cities that formed part of their project work for the Urban Consultancy Project, the final output of the programme. 

Sultan AlKhuraissi, Class of 2024-26, explores how Riyadh Metro can go beyond mobility to shape active, climate-responsive urban districts in a city historically shaped by automobile-oriented growth.

Focusing on three distinct station contexts—KAFD as a central business district, STC/Olaya as a mixed-use corridor, and Qasr AlHokm as a heritage district—the project combines spatial analysis, policy review, expert interviews, and practitioner engagement to move from theory to delivery.

The project reflects a core strength of the Executive MSc in Cities programme: connecting urban theory with practical delivery. Understanding the city as a system means not only diagnosing challenges, but asking what can realistically be delivered, by whom, and with what impact.

Read more here: https://lnkd.in/efchxbHK 

#LSECities #ExecutiveMScInCities

items.content_text:
Shooting – Hawkins Oak Lane
 The Chattanooga Police Department (CPD) is investigating a domestic-related shooting that occurred on July 16, 2026 just prior to 2:00 a.m. at a residence on Hawkins Oak Lane.
 Upon arrival, officers located an adult female with a gunshot wound. She was taken to a local hospital by Hamilton County EMS for treatment of a non-life-threatening injury. 
 The preliminary investigation determined the incident stemmed from a domestic dispute between the victim and suspect. During the encounter, the suspect discharged a firearm, striking the victim. The suspect fled the scene before officers arrived. 
 CPD Special Victims Unit and Crime Scene Units responded to investigate and process the scene.

items.title: This summer, our hearts are overflowing with gratitude for the incredible volunteers who joined forces with the N.I.C.E....
items.content_text:
This summer, our hearts are overflowing with gratitude for the incredible volunteers who joined forces with the N.I.C.E. team to love on our community! 💪🏼❤
 We were so inspired by the 100 amazing folks who rolled up their sleeves to completely transform five CNE rental properties with fresh landscaping and paint. Right alongside them, 80 dedicated volunteers from YM360 and Trinity Baptist Church all the way from Philadelphia, Mississippi, hit the pavement to complete BUKI scoring assessments across the entirety of the Oak Grove and Ridgedale neighborhoods.
 But this experience was about so much more than data and assessments. These volunteers trained in the Buki methodology and walked away with hands-on community-building experience to carry back home. What they learned here—that showing up for your neighbors and investing in your community truly changes everything—is a spark we know will ignite this same beautiful mission wherever they go next.
 Thank you all for bringing your energy, your hard work, and your hearts to our neighborhoods this summer! ☀

items.title: HCSO Investigating Juvenile Pedestrian Hit by Vehicle Hamilton County, TN – On the evening of July 15, 2026, HCSO deputi...
items.content_text:
HCSO Investigating Juvenile Pedestrian Hit by Vehicle
 Hamilton County, TN – On the evening of July 15, 2026, HCSO deputies responded to a traffic incident at the 7200 block of Hixson Pike involving a 13-year-old struck by a vehicle.
 Deputies determined a truck pulling a trailer loaded with lawn equipment stopped in a travel lane due to a weed eater becoming loose. The driver advised the juvenile passenger to exit the vehicle and secure the equipment.
 A second vehicle collided with the stopped vehicle while the juvenile was attempting to secure the trailer, and the juvenile was transported to the hospital by ambulance with non-life threatening injuries.
 The HCSO Traffic Unit is investigating, and charges may follow pending the outcome.
 This information is preliminary and subject to change. No further details are available.

items.title: The Tennessee Department of Health recently confirmed the state’s first two measles cases in 20 years. While these cases...
items.content_text:
The Tennessee Department of Health recently confirmed the state’s first two measles cases in 20 years. While these cases are located more than two hours away from Chattanooga, this milestone serves as a helpful reminder of how easily the virus can spread.
 Because measles is highly contagious, it can actually spread before someone even realizes they are sick.
 Key facts to keep in mind:
 Delayed Symptoms: Signs of measles can take days to appear after exposure, but the virus spreads very rapidly in under-vaccinated communities.
 What to Do: If you or a family member ever suspects exposure, contact your healthcare provider right away.
 Stay informed and protected:
 • Know the signs: View the CDC's quick-reference infographic: https://www.cdc.gov/…/measles-isnt-just-a-little-rash-infog…
 • Check your records: If you or your children need to catch up on the MMR vaccine, you can schedule an appointment locally with the Hamilton County Health Department at (423) 209-8340.
